title: 10 best herbal tinctures for flu

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with flu because its active compound, echinacein, has potent antiviral properties that target the influenza virus. When taken at the onset of symptoms, the tincture can help reduce the severity and duration of the illness by inhibiting viral replication and promoting a healthy immune response. Additionally, coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds may alleviate fever, cough, and congestion associated with the flu, making it a valuable natural remedy to combat this seasonal menace.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains antiviral compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses like the influenza virus, helping to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in Kansas Coneflower have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate fever, headache, and body aches associated with the flu, while also reducing inflammation in the lungs and airways.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower has been traditionally used to stimulate the immune system and increase resistance to infection, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against the flu virus and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Alkalamines: These compounds, specifically echinacoside and caftaric acid, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms by reducing inflammation in the body.

Iridoid Glycosides: Echinacea angustifolia tinctures contain iridoid glycosides like echinacoside, which have been found to stimulate the immune system, helping the body fight off flu viruses and infections.

Caffeic Acid Phenylethyl Ester (CAPE): CAPE, a phenolic compound found in Echinacea angustifolia, has been reported to possess antiviral properties, which can help combat flu viruses and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are primarily used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, such as echinatin and echinacoside, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, are often used as they contain a higher concentration of immunomodulatory compounds compared to the roots.
Flowers: Echinacea angustifolia flowers are used in some tinctures due to their content of glycosides and polyphenols, which may have antiviral and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall or early spring when the plant is dormant.
Chop 1 part of echinacea angustifolia roots into small pieces for better extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with flu because of its antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the replication of various viruses, including influenza A and B. Additionally, licorice tincture's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and recover from the flu. Its anti-inflammatory effects also help to alleviate fever, cough, and body aches associated with the flu.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and body, alleviating symptoms such as sore throats, fever, and fatigue.

Immune system support: Licorice tincture contains antioxidants and polyphenols that boost the immune system's natural defense against infections like the flu, helping to shorten the duration and severity of the illness.
Expectorant properties: The glycyrrhizin in licorice root helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion, coughs, and chest tightness associated with the flu.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate the symptoms of flu by reducing inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract.
Licopyranocoumarin: Licopyranocoumarin has antiviral properties that help combat the influenza virus by inhibiting its replication and spread in the body.
Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the flu virus, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are used to make tinctures for flu because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for flu because they are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for flu because they contain compounds such as glycosides and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra and chop it into small pieces for tincture preparation.
Combine the chopped root with 2 parts of vodka or 75 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove the solids.
Discard the solids and store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters tinctures helps with flu because it contains a blend of potent herbs that work together to soothe and calm the body. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce fever and relieve congestion, while its antiviral compounds attack the flu virus, halting its spread through the body. Additionally, the bitter taste stimulates digestion and boosts immune function, supporting the body's natural defense against infection. As a result, King of bitters tinctures can provide effective relief from flu symptoms, helping you recover faster and stronger.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: The tannins and polyphenols in King of Bitters tincture have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against influenza viruses, helping to prevent the replication and spread of the virus.

Immunomodulatory effects: The bioactive compounds in King of Bitters tincture, such as glycosides and flavonoids, can modulate immune responses by activating natural killer cells and increasing antibody production, which helps the body fight off the flu virus more effectively.

Anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties: The volatile oils and saponins in King of Bitters tincture have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic activities, which can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as headache, muscle aches, and fatigue associated with the flu.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This labdane diterpenoid helps with flu by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Andrographolide glucosides: These compounds, particularly andrographolide-14-O-β-D-glucopyranoside, contribute to the antiviral and immunomodulatory activities of Andrographis paniculata, helping to boost the body's defenses against flu viruses.

Wogonin: A flavone glycoside present in Andrographis paniculata, wogonin has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of the bioactive compound andrographolide, which is responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of andrographolide compared to the leaves, making them a preferred choice for tincture production.
Stems: The stems of Andrographis paniculata also contain andrographolide, although in smaller amounts compared to the leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Use a 1:5 ratio to combine 30 grams of dried andrographis paniculata with 150 ml of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the dark at room temperature for 2 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store the tincture in a dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Woad tinctures helps with flu because they contain a concentrated form of botanicals that work synergistically to combat viral infections. Echinacea, for example, stimulates the immune system to produce white blood cells that fight off invading viruses, while elderberry's anthocyanins have antiviral properties that inhibit the spread of infection. The tinctures' antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds also help reduce fever and soothe respiratory symptoms, making them a natural and effective way to alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Echinacea, for example, has been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of the flu.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal ingredients like ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce fever, ease congestion, and soothe a sore throat.

Immune-modulating properties: Herbs like elderberry and yarrow contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off infections and promote recovery from flu.
remedy_constituents: Isatisin: A phenolic glycoside that helps with flu by exhibiting antiviral properties, specifically inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus and reducing the severity of flu symptoms.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that helps with flu by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune response, and inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus.

Saponins: A group of triterpenoid compounds with immunomodulatory and antiviral properties that help with flu by enhancing the immune response, reducing inflammation, and inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Isatis tinctoria leaves are used to make tinctures due to their high content of isothiocyanates, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Isatis tinctoria roots are used in tinctures to take advantage of their immunomodulatory effects, which help to stimulate the immune system and fight off infections.

Seeds: Isatis tinctoria seeds are used to make tinctures for their high content of isothiocyanates and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest isatis tinctoria roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Clean the harvested plant material by rinsing it with pure water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the isatis tinctoria plant material in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 8 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried isatis tinctoria plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every few days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with flu because of its natural antiviral properties, particularly against the influenza virus. Echinacea purpurea, the herb used to make purple coneflower tincture, has been shown to stimulate the immune system and increase antibody production, helping to fight off infection. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and sore throats. By taking a purple coneflower tincture during the onset of flu, individuals may experience reduced duration and severity of symptoms, promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Purple coneflower tincture has been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of the flu.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms like sore throat, congestion, and coughing.

Immunomodulatory properties: Purple coneflower tincture has been found to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, which helps fight off the flu virus and prevent secondary infections.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to boost the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells, which fight off viral infections like the flu.
Echinacosides: These caffeic acid derivatives have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
Polysaccharides (Arabinogalactan): These complex carbohydrates stimulate the production of interferon, a protein that helps to activate the immune system and fight off viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of alkylamides and other compounds that enhance immune function and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Utilized for their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate flu symptoms.
Leaves: Employed for their ability to provide antioxidant properties and support immune function, helping to combat flu and other respiratory infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a single layer at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part dried echinacea purpurea with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with flu because of its natural antiviral properties, which help to combat the infection and alleviate symptoms. The tincture contains isosterols, a group of compounds that have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against influenza viruses. Additionally, dandelion has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and soothe sore throats and coughs. By incorporating herbal dandelion tinctures into one's regimen, individuals can potentially reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture helps to flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as congestion, fever, and body aches associated with the flu.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by viral infections like the flu.

Antiviral properties: Some studies suggest that dandelion contains compounds that exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including those responsible for the common cold and flu, helping to boost the immune system and prevent infection.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms by minimizing inflammation in the body.

Luteolin: This flavonoid phenolic compound has potent antiviral and antioxidant properties, making it effective in preventing the replication of flu viruses and reducing oxidative stress caused by the infection.

Taraxacum officinale flavonoids: The flavonoid glycosides present in dandelion tinctures, such as apigenin and luteolin, exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the body's immune response to fight off flu viruses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of taraxasterol, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used due to their rich content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, useful in treating flu symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have been found to have immunomodulatory and antiviral properties, helping to combat flu viruses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers by cutting them at the base of the plant with scissors.
Dry the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried taraxacum officinale with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese milkweed tinctures helps with flu because of its antiviral properties, which help to combat the infection by inhibiting the replication of viruses in the body. The tincture's bioactive compounds, such as saponins and alkaloids, also have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce congestion, coughing, and sore throat associated with flu. Additionally, milkweed tinctures can help boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. By incorporating Chinese milkweed tinctures into their treatment plan, individuals may experience faster recovery and reduced risk of complications from the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: The saponins present in Milkweed, such as asclepiadin and calotropin, have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against various viruses, including the influenza virus, helping to reduce its replication and severity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The triterpenoids found in Milkweed, like asclepic acid, possess anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, coughing, and sore throat associated with the flu by reducing inflammation and swelling in the body.

Immune system support: Milkweed contains various bioactive compounds that have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to respond to viral infections like the flu, which can help the body fight off the infection more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: An antioxidant flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common factors in the development and progression of flu.
Saponins: A group of glycosides with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help combat viral infections and alleviate flu symptoms.

Triterpenoids: A class of compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which could help regulate the immune system and alleviate flu-related inflammation and oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, which are believed to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help reduce fever and alleviate flu symptoms.
Stems: Rich in saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and may help combat flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia pekinensis roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces weighing about 500 grams and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried euphorbia pekinensis root pieces with 70 vodka and a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts solvent in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 months shaking it every week to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids to obtain the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with flu because of its impressive antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of various viruses and bacteria that cause respiratory infections. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce congestion, coughing, and fever associated with flu symptoms. Additionally, goldenrod's expectorant qualities help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and alleviate sinus pressure and chest tightness. As a result, goldenrod tinctures offer natural relief from the uncomfortable and debilitating effects of the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Goldenrod tincture contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to inhibit the replication of flu viruses in the body.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in goldenrod tincture, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help to reduce symptoms like fever, congestion, and body aches associated with the flu.

Mucolytic activity: Goldenrod tincture contains mucolytic compounds that help to break down mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel from the lungs and airways, which can alleviate coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of flu, such as reducing fever and relieving body aches.
Triterpenoids: They exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat viral infections, such as the flu, by inhibiting the replication of the virus and reducing the severity of symptoms.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system, reduce the severity of flu symptoms, and promote overall recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties to help relieve respiratory symptoms associated with the flu.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties to help combat flu symptoms.
Roots: They are used for their antiseptic and immunomodulatory properties to help boost the immune system and fight off flu viruses.
remedy_recipe: Collect 30-40 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers from a trusted source for future use.
Combine 30-40 grams of dried solidago virgaurea flowers with 500 milliliters of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years in airtight glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle tinctures helps with flu because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The plant's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. This can help alleviate symptoms like fever, coughing, and congestion, making it easier for the body to recover from the flu. Additionally, Japanese honeysuckle tinctures may also boost the immune system, helping to prevent future infections.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Japanese Honeysuckle tincture contains antiviral compounds that help to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms.

Immune-boosting properties: The tincture's immunomodulatory effects help to stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, enhancing its ability to fight off infection and reduce inflammation associated with the flu.
Antipyretic properties: Japanese Honeysuckle contains compounds that can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as headache, sore throat, and fatigue commonly associated with influenza.
remedy_constituents: Kaempferol: This flavonoid helps alleviate flu symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can aid in reducing the severity of flu-related inflammation and viral replication.
Loniceric acid: This phenolic acid has been shown to possess antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system and combat viral infections such as the flu.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate flu symptoms.
Flowers: Used for their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, which may help combat flu viruses.
Barks: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe flu-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lonicera japonica flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the plant material by gently rinsing it with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the cleaned plant material with 500ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help combat the underlying infection. The tincture's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. By targeting the root cause of the illness, maidenhair tree tinctures can help alleviate symptoms like coughing, fever, and congestion, promoting a faster recovery from the flu.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the flu virus, reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory congestion, coughing, and body aches associated with the flu.

Immune system support: Maidenhair Tree has been traditionally used to enhance immune function, and its extracts have been shown to increase the production of cytokines, which fight off infections and reduce the duration of illness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by the flu virus, thereby alleviating symptoms such as fever and body aches.

Terpenoids: Specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help inhibit the replication of the flu virus and reduce the severity of symptoms such as cough and congestion.

Proanthocyanidins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties, which help enhance the body's natural defense against the flu virus and reduce the duration and severity of the illness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate flu symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgotoxin, a compound that has shown potential in reducing the severity of flu symptoms by inhibiting the replication of the flu virus.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can aid in recovering from flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the leaves in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Combine the powdered ginkgo biloba with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily then strain and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with flu because of its antiviral properties, which can help combat the influenza virus and alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. The herb's flavonoids and polyphenols work to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. Additionally, elder tinctures' expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against several types of viruses, including those that cause the flu, which helps to reduce the severity and duration of the illness.

Immune system stimulation: Elder tincture contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, which can help fight off viral infections like the flu.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms such as body aches and headaches, and prevent complications like pneumonia.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds and saponins, making them effective in reducing fever and relieving congestion associated with the flu.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes have antimicrobial properties that help combat viral infections like the flu, and their sweet flavor makes them a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.

Barks: The barks of elder trees contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, making them useful in treating flu symptoms such as sore throats and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the plant material by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading it out to air dry.
Cut the dried plant material into small pieces and place 20% of the weight in a clean glass jar with 80% vodka or ethanol at 40 proof.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily, before straining through cheesecloth.
Store the tincture in a cool dark bottle and take 20-30 drops three times a day as needed, or 1/4 teaspoon in tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Amur corktree tinctures helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat viral infections that cause flu. The herb contains phlobaphenes, a group of compounds that have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against influenza viruses. Additionally, Amur corktree has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, coughing, and body aches associated with the flu. By incorporating Amur corktree tinctures into one's natural remedy routine, individuals can potentially reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Amur Corktree tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral effects against various flu viruses, including influenza A and B, which can help reduce the severity and duration of the illness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches associated with flu by reducing inflammation in the body.
Immune system support: Amur Corktree contains bioactive compounds that can stimulate the immune system to produce interferons, which are proteins that help fight off viral infections like the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the flu.
Barks: Contain compounds like phellodendrin, which has antiviral properties and helps combat the viral replication of the flu virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh phellodendron amurense leaves at peak potency for optimal medicinal value.
Combine 100 grams of fresh phellodendron amurense leaves with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing for gentle maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another glass jar discarding the solids after one hour.
Store the filtered tincture in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tickseed tinctures helps with flu because it contains potent compounds that work to combat the viral infection. The tannins and flavonoids present in tickseed have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of the virus and reducing its severity. Additionally, tickseed's anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion, making it a valuable natural remedy for flu relief. remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Tickseed tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat the influenza virus and reduce its replication in the body.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches associated with the flu by reducing inflammation in the body.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a rich source of bioactive compounds that help to stimulate the immune system and alleviate congestion, making them effective in treating flu symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and ease body aches associated with the flu, while also providing antioxidant support.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia hirta leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried but before scorching heat.
Wash the plant material gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with clean cloth.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and mix with 70 vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1 part herb to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in glass jar with lid in dark place for four to six weeks shaking daily to extract constituents.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass bottle discard solids and store tincture in dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Porter's lovage tinctures helps with flu because its unique blend of herbal extracts, including lovage root, echinacea, and ginger, provides a natural boost to the immune system. Lovage root has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate congestion, while echinacea enhances the body's ability to fight off infections. Ginger adds warmth and comfort to the chest and throat, relieving coughs and sore throats. By combining these herbs in a tincture form, Porter's lovage tinctures offers a powerful and soothing remedy for flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Lovage tincture helps to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion caused by the flu.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lovage contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Antiviral properties: Lovage has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including influenza A and B, which may help prevent or reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages and saponins, roots are effective in soothing coughs and reducing inflammation associated with flu.
Rhyzomes: Contains sesquiterpenes, which have antibacterial properties, helping to combat secondary infections that often accompany the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried ligusticum porteri roots and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and measure out 20-30 grams for every 100 milliliters of vodka.
Combine the chopped roots with 100 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture in a dark cool place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily for at least two minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with flu because its essential oils, menthol and menthone, have natural decongestant properties that can ease congestion and coughs. The tincture's cooling sensation also helps to reduce fever and soothe a sore throat. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate body aches and headaches often associated with the flu. By promoting expectoration, peppermint tinctures can also help to clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for faster recovery and relief from flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms like fever, headache, and sore throat.
Decongestant property: Peppermint's menthol content helps to break up mucus and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a stuffy nose and chest.
Digestive-soothing property: Peppermint's calming effect on the digestive tract can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and abdominal discomfort that often accompany flu-like illnesses.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, peppermint roots help to reduce fever and relieve body aches associated with the flu.
Leaves: The leaves contain menthol and other volatile oils that provide expectorant properties, helping to ease congestion and coughing caused by the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of high quality and dry them completely for at least 48 hours.
Combine 1 part dried peppermint leaves with 3 parts vodka in a clean glass jar for a ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking occasionally every day or two.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme tinctures helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's essential oils, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses, reducing the severity of symptoms like coughing, sneezing, and fatigue. Additionally, creeping thyme's antioxidant compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate flu-like symptoms. By taking a creeping thyme tincture, individuals may experience faster recovery times and reduced risk of complications from the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Creeping thyme tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain strains of the influenza virus, helping to combat and prevent the spread of infection.

Mucolytic properties: The saponins present in creeping thyme tincture help break down mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving congestion and coughing associated with flu.

Immune-boosting properties: Creeping thyme contains flavonoids and other compounds that have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells, which helps fight off the infection and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, thyme roots have been traditionally used to combat respiratory infections like the flu.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which can help to relieve congestion and coughing associated with flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus serpyllum leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and combine them in a clean glass jar with 50% vodka and 50% water.
Place the jar in a dark cabinet for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to allow for maceration and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Store the final thymus serpyllum tincture in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) for up to 2 years, using it as needed to help alleviate flu symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen tinctures helps with flu because they contain salvia miltiorrhiza, a plant-based compound that has natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. When taken as an herbal supplement, danshen tincture can help to reduce fever, alleviate congestion, and soothe sore throats. Its antimicrobial effects may also aid in fighting off the underlying infection, allowing the body to recover faster and more effectively from the flu virus. Additionally, danshen's antioxidant properties may help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage caused by the virus.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Danshen tincture helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body aches associated with the flu.

Antiviral properties: The tincture contains compounds like salvianolic acid B, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including influenza viruses, thereby helping to combat the infection.
Immune-boosting properties: Danshen tincture is believed to stimulate the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, danshen roots have been traditionally used to treat fever and cough associated with flu, making them a popular choice for medicinal tincture production.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the danshen plant is rich in saponins and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties when used in flu tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia miltiorrhiza roots weighing approximately one kilogram for subsequent drying.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for three hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and pack them tightly into a clean glass jar leaving one inch of headspace.
Fill the jar with high-proof vodka or ethanol reaching the rim and let it steep in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking weekly.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with flu because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, cough, and congestion. The herb's bioactive compounds, including angelic acid and isoangelicin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of the illness. Additionally, angelica tinctures can help soothe a sore throat and calm a hacking cough, providing relief from uncomfortable flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like congestion and coughing.

Antiviral properties: The tincture contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against influenza virus strains, potentially reducing the severity and duration of the flu.

Expectorant properties: Angelica's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to expel excess phlegm and relieving symptoms like coughing and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the angelica plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including congestion and coughs associated with the flu.
Barks: Angelica barks contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, providing relief from flu-related respiratory symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried angelica archangelica roots in autumn when ripe for optimal potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for six to eight weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after two hours preparation.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 2-3°c for up to three years.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for flu are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate flu symptoms by boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and relieving congestion. The best herbal tinctures for flu include Echinacea purpurea, Echinacea angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Sambucus nigra, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea pallida, Andrographis paniculata, Ginkgo biloba, and Lonicera japonica. If left untreated, flu can lead to missed workdays, lost productivity, and strained relationships due to exhaustion and discomfort, ultimately affecting one's overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to flu that are treated with herbal tinctures are common colds, bronchitis, and sinus infections. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like elderberry, echinacea, and goldenseal have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and fatigue. These remedies can provide relief from respiratory issues without the side effects associated with over-the-counter medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with flu is a blend of Elderberry, Echinacea, and Ginger. Elderberry tincture helps reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, while Echinacea tincture boosts the immune system to fight off the infection. Ginger tincture aids in reducing fever and nausea. Additionally, incorporating a small amount of Licorice Root tincture can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation. This combination of tinctures can help alleviate flu symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with flu because it has been traditionally used to boost the immune system and reduce the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms. The tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate congestion and sore throats. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying viral infection. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea tinctures may help to alleviate flu symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to combat the underlying viral and bacterial infections that cause the flu.
Icariin: Exhibits immunomodulatory effects, stimulating the body's natural defense mechanisms to fight off the flu virus.
Caffeic acid: Possesses antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are most commonly used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to have immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

(Optional) Leaves: Leaves are sometimes included, as they contain a smaller amount of alkaloids and flavonoids compared to roots and flowers, but can still contribute to the overall potency of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at the peak of their potency usually in late summer or early fall season.
Chop 1 part of the plant material to 2 parts of 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the plant material in the solvent for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the echinacea tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid away from heat and light sources indefinitely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia tinctures helps with flu because of its potent immunomodulatory properties. It contains alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives that stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells. This response enables the body to fight off viral infections more effectively. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as sore throats and body aches, allowing individuals to recover faster. Its antimicrobial properties also help prevent the spread of the infection.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms to combat flu viruses.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, body aches, and congestion associated with the flu.

Caffeic Acid: Caffeic acid has been demonstrated to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and viral replication, contributing to the prevention and treatment of the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate flu symptoms.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help fight flu-causing pathogens.
Flowers: They are a rich source of echinacosides and other glycosides that have immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's natural defense against flu viruses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late fall or early spring when they are at their highest potency.
Chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped echinacea roots with 2 parts vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place or 1-2 weeks in a warm place.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with flu because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing congestion and relieving respiratory symptoms. The tincture's active ingredients, including gingerols and shogaols, help to combat viral infections and alleviate fever, while its warming and soothing effects provide relief from body aches and headaches. Additionally, ginger's natural decongestant properties help to ease coughs and sinus pressure, making it a popular remedy for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues associated with flu by acting as an anti-inflammatory and expectorant.
Shogaols: Exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat flu-causing viruses and bacteria, reducing the severity and duration of the illness.
Gingerol-6 (Gingerdiol): Possesses antiviral properties that can inhibit the replication of flu viruses, reducing the risk of transmission and severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part of ginger to make tinctures for flu due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: Roots are another commonly used part of ginger to make tinctures for flu as they contain similar bioactive compounds to rhyzomes, which help to alleviate flu symptoms.

Stems: Stems are less commonly used but still used in some tinctures for flu due to their ability to provide additional bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which contribute to their medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale rhizomes in the fall after the foliage has died back to utilize the roots medicinal properties.
Clean and dry the rhizomes by placing them in a single layer on a baking sheet for 24 hours.
Chop the dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase the surface area.
Steep 500 grams of chopped rhizomes in 2 liters of vodka at a ratio of 1:4 for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with flu because of its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The licorice root's active compounds, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory symptoms associated with flu. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been found to exhibit antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of viral particles and helping to shorten the duration of the flu. This makes it a valuable natural remedy for supporting the body's defense against flu viruses.
remedy_constituents: Licorice saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as sore throat and body aches.
Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which help to boost the body's immune response and fight off the flu virus.
Glycyrrhetic acid: This triterpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms and prevent the spread of the virus in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used in tinctures for flu due to their ability to help reduce fever and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make tinctures for flu as they contain compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning to capture optimal essential oil and bioactive compound concentrations.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area at 35-40°c for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix 1 part of the ground root powder with 2 parts of 60% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with flu because it is rich in antiviral compounds, particularly elderberry flavonoids and sambubiosides, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of the influenza virus. The tincture's high concentration of anthocyanins also gives it potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and body aches. By boosting the immune system and reducing viral load, Sambucus nigra tinctures can help alleviate flu symptoms and potentially shorten the duration of the illness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit potent antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of flu viruses and aiding in the recovery process.
Sambunigrin: This glycoside has been shown to have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate flu symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Sambucus nigra are used to make tinctures for flu because they contain antiviral properties that help combat the virus.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra are used to make tinctures for flu because they exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that reduce symptoms associated with flu.
Roots: The roots of Sambucus nigra are used to make tinctures for flu because they have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's immune system to fight off the flu.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at the peak of bloom for optimal potency and aroma.
Chop 1 part of the plant material into small pieces and add to a clean glass jar.
Add 2 parts of a solvent like 80 proof vodka to the jar to facilitate extraction.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to a clean dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as cineole and eucalyptol, work together to combat the influenza virus and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. This leads to relief from congestion, coughing, and fever. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tinctures have been shown to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off the flu more effectively. As a result, it can be a valuable natural remedy for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as a bronchodilator and decongestant, helping to relieve respiratory symptoms such as congestion and cough associated with flu.
β-Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms and prevent secondary infections.
Limonene: Possesses antimicrobial and expectorant properties, aiding in the relief of respiratory tract congestion and cough, and helping to reduce the risk of complications from flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are the most commonly used part to make tinctures for the flu because they contain the highest concentration of eucalyptol, a compound with decongestant and antimicrobial properties.

Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are also widely used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help combat flu symptoms.

Stems: Eucalyptus globulus stems are another commonly used part for tincture production, as they contain a combination of essential oils, including eucalyptol, that help relieve respiratory issues and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh leaves of the plant in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea pallida tinctures helps with flu because it stimulates the immune system, increasing its ability to fight off infections. It contains alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea pallida tinctures have been shown to inhibit the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the flu's debilitating symptoms, such as fever, body aches, and fatigue. This natural remedy may help alleviate flu symptoms and support overall health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it easier for the body to fight off flu viruses.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
Phenylethanoids: Phenylethanoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect cells from damage caused by flu viruses and reduce the risk of secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Echinacea pallida.
Leaves: They are a more accessible and less expensive part to harvest, making them a practical choice for tincture production.
Buds: They are a concentrated source of plant compounds, allowing for a more potent tincture with a smaller amount of plant material.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part echinacea pallida roots and 2 parts aerial parts by hand in early morning or late afternoon when the plant is dry.
Cut the harvested material into small pieces using sharp knives or pruning shears to ensure even extraction.
Combine the cut plant material with 70-80% ethanol in a ratio of 1:2 to 1:3 in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional shaking and gentle stirring to ensure complete extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata tinctures helps with flu because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. It contains compounds like andrographolide, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of flu viruses, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. By boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Andrographis paniculata tinctures can help alleviate flu symptoms such as fever, body aches, and fatigue. Additionally, it has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine to treat respiratory infections, making it a popular natural remedy for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Andrographolide: This labdane diterpenoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
Neoadrographolide: This labdane diterpenoid has immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response to the flu virus and reduce the risk of complications.
Andrographisolide: This labdane diterpenoid has antiviral properties that help inhibit the replication of the flu virus, reducing the duration and severity of the illness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of andrographolide, a key bioactive compound responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Roots: The roots of Andrographis paniculata are rich in andrographolide, which is often used to treat various health issues, including flu.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain a smaller amount of andrographolide compared to the leaves and roots, but are still used for their medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers during the early morning when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours to prevent degradation.
Combine 1:5 ratio of dried plant material to 95% ethanol in a clean glass container and allow it to macerate for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle.
Store the andrographis paniculata tincture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow the flavors to mature slowly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with flu because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba extract have been shown to help alleviate flu symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and boosting the immune system. By enhancing oxygen delivery to the body's cells, Ginkgo biloba tinctures may help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as fever, fatigue, and congestion, making it a popular natural remedy for flu relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which may alleviate flu symptoms such as congestion and fever.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to combat oxidative stress and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
Bilobalide: This compound has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and muscle pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba, leaves contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate flu symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make tinctures, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help combat viral infections and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain glycosides and terpenoids that can help boost the immune system and fight off flu viruses, making it a popular choice for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part fresh or dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Combine the ginkgo biloba material with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use within 2 years after preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lonicera japonica tinctures helps with flu because it contains compounds that have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to combat the flu virus and reduce its severity. By inhibiting viral replication and reducing inflammation, Lonicera japonica tincture can help alleviate symptoms like fever, sore throat, and body aches. This natural remedy can provide relief and support the body's immune system during the recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This anti-inflammatory constituent helps reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with flu.
Caryophyllene: This anti-inflammatory sesquiterpene constituent has been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help combat the flu virus.
Naringenin: This flavonoid constituent has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms and support the immune system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Contain antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to combat flu symptoms and support respiratory health.
Buds: Rich in glycosides and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and other flu-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh lonicera japonica flowers and leaves on a sunny morning in late spring.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 40c for 6-8 hours or in shade for 7-10 days.
Combine 250g of dried lonicera japonica flowers and leaves with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and then strain through cheesecloth.
Store the tincture in a clean dark glass bottle at room temperature and let it mature for 2-3 months.
